name: Rice Blast
symptoms: Diamond-shaped lesions on leaves, white/gray center with brown border, yellowing around spots, neck rot in severe cases
treatment: Tricyclazole 75 WP @ 0.6g/L water. Spray 2-3 times at 10-day intervals.
prevention: Use BPT 5204 or resistant varieties. Balanced NPK. Avoid water stress.

name: Late Blight
symptoms: Water-soaked pale green spots on leaves, white cottony growth on underside, brown-black lesions that spread rapidly
treatment: Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2.5g/L OR Metalaxyl + Mancozeb @ 2g/L. Spray every 7 days.
prevention: Use certified disease-free seed. Avoid planting in waterlogged areas.

name: Wheat Rust
symptoms: Orange-yellow or brown powdery pustules on leaves, stripe pattern, premature leaf drying
treatment: Propiconazole 25 EC @ 1ml/L. Apply at first sign. Repeat after 15 days.
prevention: Sow rust-resistant varieties (HD 2967, PBW 343). Early sowing before October.

name: Fusarium Wilt
symptoms: Sudden yellowing and wilting of leaves, brown discolouration inside stem, one-sided wilting
treatment: Carbendazim 50 WP @ 1g/L as soil drench around plant base.
prevention: Crop rotation — do not plant same family for 3 years. Use wilt-tolerant varieties.

name: Mosaic Virus
symptoms: Alternating yellow-green mosaic pattern on leaves, leaf curling and distortion, stunted growth
treatment: No chemical cure. Control aphid vectors: Imidacloprid 17.8 SL @ 0.5ml/L.
prevention: Use virus-indexed seed. Control aphids from day 1. Yellow sticky traps.

name: Downy Mildew
symptoms: Yellow oily patches on upper leaf surface, whitish-gray downy growth on undersides
treatment: Metalaxyl + Mancozeb @ 2.5g/L. Spray early morning at 7-day intervals.
prevention: Prune for airflow. Apply preventive copper spray before monsoon.

name: Yellow Mosaic Virus
symptoms: Bright yellow patches on leaves, alternating green-yellow mosaic, small distorted leaves
treatment: Thiamethoxam 25 WG @ 0.3g/L to control whitefly vector. Spray every 10 days.
prevention: Early sowing before June 15 for Kharif. Use YMV-resistant varieties (JS 335).

name: Stem Borer
symptoms: Dead heart in young plants, holes in stem with frass, leaf window feeding
treatment: Chlorpyrifos 20 EC @ 2.5ml/L foliar spray. Carbofuran 3G @ 20 kg/ha in whorl.
prevention: Crop rotation. Early planting before June 15. Light traps for adult moths.

name: Anthracnose
symptoms: Dark sunken lesions on fruits, black spots on leaves with yellow halo, twig dieback
treatment: Copper Oxychloride 50 WP @ 3g/L spray during flowering. Mancozeb as alternate.
prevention: Prune dead wood. Orchard sanitation. Remove mummified fruits.

name: Covered Smut
symptoms: Grain replaced by black powdery mass, smutted heads appear at flowering, foul odor
treatment: Seed treatment: Carboxin 75 WP + Thiram 75 WP @ 2.5g/kg seed.
prevention: Seed treatment is the ONLY effective control. Do before every sowing.
